frame_analyses.description: The trainee is preparing to load the needle with the suture.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Both instruments are positioned above the target area.

frame_analyses.description: The suture is being placed through the first mark on the Penrose drain.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Correct angling observed for precise placement.

frame_analyses.description: The suture is being placed through the second mark.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Needle passes cleanly through the mark.

frame_analyses.description: The trainee is executing the surgeon's knot with a double throw.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: The right hand is primarily used for this throw.

frame_analyses.description: A single throw is being tied as the second throw of the knot.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Hand switch to left hand is apparent from throw one.

frame_analyses.description: The final single throw is being performed.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Switches back to the right hand from the left.

frame_analyses.description: Both suture ends are being cut to complete the task.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Instruments are positioned accurately to trim close to the knot.

frame_analyses.description: The task is completed with both suture ends cut.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Tools have been removed; the knot appears intact.

knot_assessments.notes: Single throw is secure with proper hand switch.

drain_assessment.assessment_note: Slit is closed without gaps, and drain remains intact.

technique_summary: The trainee successfully completed the task with minimal deviation in suture placement. The knot was secure, and the drain showed no visible gaps post-procedure.
improvement_suggestions: Focus on reducing suture placement deviation further.
Ensure consistent hand switches for all throws to improve efficiency.
strengths: The knots were tied securely, maintaining the integrity of the closure.
Good hand switch technique in knot tying phases.
